CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 57653868) FILED BY DEFENDANT LAYALY GRILL, INC. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS ANTON GONCHARUK AND IGOR EVSEEV'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CLAIMS FOR FRAUD HAVE HEIGHTENED PLEADING REQUIREMENTS. (COMMITTEE ON CHILDREN'S TELEVISION, INC. V. GENERAL FOODS CORP. (1983) 35 CAL.3D 197, 218-219.) PLAINTIFFS MAY HAVE A SCHEME TO REPRESENT THEMSELVES AS INDEPENDENT CONTRACTORS IN ORDER TO SUE, BUT ON ITS FACE DEFENDANTS' CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR FRAUD FAILS FOR LACK OF SPECIFICITY. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO OFFER " 'FACTS WHICH SHOW HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE TENDERED.' [CITATION.]" (LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) THE ELEMENTS OF A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INDEMNITY ARE (1) A SHOWING OF FAULT ON THE PART OF THE INDEMNITOR AND (2) RESULTING DAMAGES TO THE INDEMNITEE FOR WHICH THE INDEMNITOR IS CONTRACTUALLY OR EQUITABLY RESPONSIBLE." (EXPRESSIONS AT RANCHO NIGUEL ASS'N V. AHMANSON DEVELOPMENTS, INC. (2001) 86 CAL.APP.4TH 1135, 1139.) DEFENDANTS FAIL TO MAKE THIS SHOWING WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY AND A DEMURRER IS THEREFORE PROPER AT THIS JUNCTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Continued To Mar-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS ANTON GONCHARUK AND IGOR EVSEEV'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Claims for fraud have heightened pleading requirements. (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 218-219.) Plaintiffs may have a scheme to represent themselves as independent contractors in order to sue, but on its face Defendants' cross-complaint for fraud fails for lack of specificity. Defendants fail to offer " 'facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.' [Citation.]" (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) The elements of a cause of action for indemnity are (1) a showing of fault on the part of the indemnitor and (2) resulting damages to the indemnitee for which the indemnitor is contractually or equitably responsible." (Expressions at Rancho Niguel Ass'n v. Ahmanson Developments, Inc. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1139.) Defendants fail to make this showing with sufficient specificity and a demurrer is therefore proper at this junction. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants To Produce Documents And Awarding Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Text Ruling

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Goncharuk's unopposed Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents against Defendants Layaly Grill, Inc and Ahmed Bursa Elsakar is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide amended, code-compliant responses to the requests for production of document in the Motion, along with responsive documents, within 30 days of the notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,000, payable within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. Order signed in open Court. (302)

MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. CHESTERFIELD A. SPAHR OF THE TO LONG & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-415-386-8136 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

Sec. 128.7 Against Defendants/Cross-Complainants Layaly Grill Inc, Ahmed Bursa Elsakars Counsel - The motion for sanctions is denied.
